title: Telegram Assistant Liveness Feedback
summary: Implemented Telegram assistant liveness feedback with delayed native typing indicators, contextual long-run progress notes, timeout messaging, edit-in-place final delivery, and coverage for timeout and edit-fallback paths.
next_action: Deploy and verify the Telegram liveness behavior in production when requested.

verification.notes: `mix precommit` passed on 2026-03-12 after wiring Telegram `sendChatAction`, the liveness session supervisor, runner delivery integration, and end-to-end assistant timing coverage.

resume_context.last_completed_step: Implemented the Telegram liveness session, connector/responder support, runner delivery integration, edit fallback behavior, timeout suppression, and passing verification coverage.
resume_context.notes: The delivered behavior keeps scope to assistant-owned inbound Telegram runs. The assistant now starts typing after a short grace period, sends one contextual progress note on long runs, edits that note into the final answer when possible, and suppresses late answers after the timeout notice.

history.note: Initial draft created from the existing Telegram webhook-router-runner path, the unified Telegram assistant spec, and the product decisions favoring delayed typing, one 7-second contextual progress note, and explicit timeout messaging.
Implementation began by adding Telegram chat-action support, a per-run liveness session supervisor, runner-aware delivery modes, and timing-focused Telegram assistant tests.
Completed the Telegram liveness implementation with delayed native typing, contextual progress notes, timeout notifications, edit fallback behavior, and passing `mix precommit` verification.
